sanskrit: तत्क्षेत्रं यच्च यादृक् च यद्विकारि यतश्च यत् । स च यो यत्प्रभावश्च तत्समासेन मे श्रृणु ॥
hindiTranslation: वह क्षेत्र जो है, जैसा है, जिन विकारोंवाला है और जिससे जो पैदा हुआ है तथा वह क्षेत्रज्ञ भी जो है और जिस प्रभाववाला है, वह सब संक्षेपमें मेरेसे सुन ॥
englishTranslation: Listen to the field, its nature, its changes, its source, and the knower of the field, along with that knower's powers, in brief.
essence: Clear seeing begins by separating the field from the one who knows it.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna is about to explain, in brief, what the field is like, how it changes, where it comes from, and what the knower of the field is like.
simpleInsight: You do not have to guess at reality; you can learn to examine it closely.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is frozen and Krishna begins teaching the difference between the body-field and the knower within it. After naming that distinction, Krishna now promises a compact explanation of the field’s nature, changes, source, and the knower’s power.
modernRelevance: You are staring at a decision that keeps changing shape: the facts, your mood, and your motives all blur together. This verse says to slow down and examine each layer clearly before you act.
translationLiteral: तत् (that) / क्षेत्रम् (the field) / यत् (which) / च (and) / यादृक् (what sort) / च (and) / यत्-विकारि (having what changes) / यतः (from where) / च (and) / यत् (what) / सः (that) / च (and) / यः (who) / यत्-प्रभावः (having what power) / च (and) / तत् (that) / समासेन (in brief) / मे (from me) / श्रृणु (hear)
